stories.title: Texas Sweeps Tennessee — Kavan Posts Fifth Career WCWS Shutout, Longhorns Return to Championship Series

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Teagan Kavan. 7 innings. 2 hits. 10 strikeouts. Zero runners past first base. ⚾

Her 5th career WCWS shutout. Her 9th career WCWS win. Both Texas program records.

The Longhorns are going back to the championship series. Three years in a row. 🤘

#HookEm #WCWS

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Tennessee never had a chance in Game 2. 🔥

Kavan was on another level — 10 Ks, two hits, not a single runner past first base.

She doesn't lose at Devon Park. That's just facts.

#WCWS #Softball

facebook.long_form:
We have seen some special WCWS performances over the years. Monday night, Teagan Kavan added her name to that conversation permanently.

In Game 2 of the Texas-Tennessee semifinal, Kavan threw a two-hit complete game shutout — striking out 10 and walking nobody. Tennessee never got a runner past first base. It was a seven-inning masterclass from a pitcher who has simply owned Devon Park.

Her stats on this stage now read: five career WCWS shutouts, nine career WCWS wins. Both are Texas program records.

And that's not all. Katie Stewart capped her program-record season with her 31st home run — the most by any player in Texas program history. The Longhorns got the sweep, advanced to the championship series for the third year in a row, and they did it in a single Monday.

Three years in a row to the finals. Let that sink in. 🤘

Who wins the championship — Texas or Texas Tech? Drop your prediction below! ⬇️
facebook.image_caption: Five career WCWS shutouts. Nine career WCWS wins. Teagan Kavan owns Devon Park. 🔥 #WCWS #HookEm

Texas Sweeps Tennessee — Kavan Posts Fifth Career WCWS Shutout
- **Classification:** FOLLOW UP (prior coverage June 1: Texas vs. Tennessee semifinals)
- **Freshness:** Verified — games played June 1, 2026. Multiple sources published June 1–2.

- Kavan record: 5th career WCWS shutout (Texas program record); 9th career WCWS win (Texas program record). Record: 28-6.
- Katie Stewart hit her 31st home run of the season in Game 2 — a program single-season record.

- Tennessee did not advance a runner past first base in Game 2.
- **Key Facts (MEDIUM confidence — single source):**

- **Why it matters to ILS audience:** Texas is the defending national champion gunning for a historic repeat, and Kavan is pitching at an all-time great level on the game's biggest stage.

stories.title: Texas Tech Tops No. 1 Alabama Twice — No. 11 Seed Canady Shuts Down Tide in Game 2

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Walk-off HR in Game 1. Complete game shutout in Game 2.

Texas Tech eliminated No. 1 Alabama TWICE on Monday. 🤯

Mia Williams walk-off (No. 26). Then Canady: 7 IP, 2 H, 0 ER, 6 K.

No. 11 seed. WCWS Finals bound. 🔴⚫

#WreckEm #WCWS

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
NiJaree Canady just silenced the No. 1 team in the country. 🙌

2 hits. 6 strikeouts. Complete game. Zero runs.

Against the top seed. In the semifinals. When it all mattered.

She's built different. 🤍

#WreckEm #WCWS

facebook.long_form:
The No. 11 seed needed two wins against the No. 1 seed to reach the championship series. Texas Tech got them both. 🤯

In Game 1, Alabama tied it in the seventh and seemed ready to punch their ticket to the finals. Then Mia Williams stepped to the plate. On the fourth pitch she saw, Williams sent her 26th home run of the season to left field for a walk-off that sent Devon Park into a frenzy.

Game 2 was Canady's moment. NiJaree Canady — maybe the most-watched player in college softball history — delivered a complete game shutout against the team with the best record in the country. Two hits. Six strikeouts. Zero runs. Jasmyn Burns' solo shot in the fourth was all the offense they needed.

A No. 11 seed beating the No. 1 seed — twice, in the same day — is one for the WCWS record books.

Texas Tech is in the finals. Believe it. 🔴⚫

Are you rooting for the Cinderella story? Let us know below! ⬇️
facebook.image_caption: Walk-off. Then a shutout. Texas Tech is headed to the WCWS Finals. 🤯 #WreckEm #WCWS

research.facts:
Texas Tech Tops No. 1 Alabama Twice — No. 11 Seed Canady Shuts Down Tide
- **Classification:** FOLLOW UP (June 1: Texas Tech vs. Alabama semifinals)
- **Freshness:** Verified — games played June 1, 2026.
- **Sources:**
- TexasTech.com: "No. 11 Texas Tech advances to WCWS Finals" (June 1, 2026) — official release
- TexasTech.com: "Williams walk-off forces WCWS Semifinal deciding game" (June 1, 2026)
- wibw.com: "NiJa Canady throws complete game shutout to send Texas Tech to WCWS finals" (June 2, 2026)
- Yahoo Sports live blog (June 1, 2026)
- **Key Facts (HIGH confidence — multi-source):**
- Game 1: Texas Tech 5, Alabama 4. Mia Williams hit walk-off home run (26th of season) to lead off the bottom of the 7th. Canady (28-6) earned win after 2⅔ innings of relief, 4 Ks, 1 ER. Alabama's Jana Young had hit a 2-run HR to tie the game 4-4 in the top of the 7th.
- Game 2: Texas Tech 2, Alabama 0. Canady threw a complete game shutout — 6 Ks, 2 hits allowed. Jasmyn Burns solo HR in 4th inning was the game's first run. Error-aided run in 7th added insurance.
- Texas Tech is No. 11 seed advancing to WCWS Finals — a historic run.
- **Key Facts (MEDIUM confidence — need second-source verification):**
- Alabama's season-ending record: reported as 54-9 per Alabama Athletics site (originally listed as 54-7 midseason). Final record needs confirmation.
- **Why it matters:** The biggest upset of the tournament — No. 11 seed eliminating the No. 1 overall seed in the semifinals.

stories.title: All-Texas WCWS Championship Series — Kavan vs. Canady, Game 1 Wednesday 8 PM ET on ESPN

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Texas vs. Texas Tech. For the national title. Again. 🏆

Game 1: Wednesday, June 3. 8 PM ET. ESPN.

Kavan vs. Canady. Defending champs vs. No. 11 seed.

This is why we watch softball. 🥎

#WCWS #HookEm #WreckEm

facebook.long_form:
Last year, it came down to Texas and Texas Tech. This year, it's happening again.

The two Lone Star State programs will meet for the national championship for the second straight year — the first WCWS finals rematch since the best-of-three format began in 2005.

Texas goes in as the defending champion gunning for a back-to-back, with Teagan Kavan pitching at a level that is historically rare for this stage. Texas Tech goes in as the No. 11 seed with NiJaree Canady at her absolute peak, coming off a complete game shutout of the No. 1 team in the country.

The all-time series favors Texas 65-13. But Texas Tech won a game in last year's finals — ending a 15-game losing streak to the Longhorns. And historically, only once has the runner-up come back the next year to win it all.

Game 1: Wednesday, June 3. 8 PM ET on ESPN. Block out your night.

Who are you watching with? 🥎
facebook.image_caption: Texas vs. Texas Tech. For the national title. Again. 🏆 Game 1: Wednesday, 8 PM ET on ESPN. #WCWS

stories.title: Karlyn Pickens' Tennessee Career Is Over — A Legend Leaves Without a Ring

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Karlyn Pickens played her last game Monday. 💔

79.4 mph — fastest pitch in softball history.
Three-time First-Team All-American.
Two-time SEC Pitcher of the Year.

Four years in Knoxville. One legacy that'll never fade. 🧡

#GBO #Softball

facebook.long_form:
On Monday at Devon Park, Karlyn Pickens played the last game of her collegiate softball career.

She spent four years at Tennessee and left as one of the most decorated pitchers the sport has ever produced. Two-time SEC Pitcher of the Year. Three-time First-Team All-American. Holder of the fastest recorded pitch in softball history: 79.4 miles per hour.

She was electric every single time she took the mound. She made people tune in to college softball who had never watched a game before. She made opponents uncomfortable just by warming up.

What she never got was a national championship. And in a sport where one day at the WCWS can define or deny a legacy, that absence will be the footnote people point to. But it shouldn't be. Karlyn Pickens was special — and she'll be remembered that way.

Thank you, Karlyn. What a run. 🧡🤍

Favorite Karlyn Pickens moment? Drop it below. ⬇️
facebook.image_caption: 79.4 mph. Three-time First-Team All-American. One legendary career. Thank you, Karlyn Pickens. 🧡 #GBO #Softball

Karlyn Pickens' Tennessee Career Ends at WCWS
- **Classification:** NEW STORY

- **Sources:**
- Rocky Top Insider: "Karlyn Pickens Reflects on Legendary Tennessee Softball Career" (June 1, 2026)

- Rocky Top Insider: Pair of Tennessee Softball Pitchers Named First-Team All-Americans (May 26, 2026)
- **Key Facts (HIGH confidence — multi-source):**
- Karlyn Pickens played all four collegiate years at Tennessee.
- Honors: SEC Freshman of the Year, two-time SEC Pitcher of the Year, three-time First-Team All-American.
- Holds the NCAA record for fastest softball pitch ever thrown: 79.4 mph (set at 2025 Super Regionals, broke her own record of 78.2 mph).
- Career ends without a national championship despite four WCWS appearances with Tennessee.
- **Why it matters:** Pickens is one of the greatest pitchers in softball history; her career conclusion is a major emotional story for the fanbase.

stories.title: Alabama Stunned — No. 1 Seed Falls to No. 11 Texas Tech in WCWS Semifinals

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
The No. 1 seed is going home. 😤

Alabama had the best record in the country. Everything set up for a title run.

Texas Tech said no — twice, in the same day.

That's why they play the games. 🥎

#RollTide #WCWS

facebook.long_form:
No team had a more dominant regular season than Alabama. They were the No. 1 overall seed at the Women's College World Series for a reason — and honestly, everything pointed to them making a run.

Jocelyn Briski was among the best pitchers in the country all year. Brooke Wells and Alexis Pupillo gave them lineup depth. Head coach Patrick Murphy has been here before — 16 WCWS appearances over his career.

But Texas Tech came in with Mia Williams swinging a hot bat and NiJaree Canady ready to be great. And on Monday, they were.

The No. 11 seed beat the No. 1 seed twice. Canady shut out Alabama in Game 2. And just like that, the Crimson Tide's season was over.

Alabama will be back. Briski could return. The program doesn't rebuild; it reloads.

But right now, it stings — and that's okay. This sport always finds a way to surprise us. 😤
facebook.image_caption: The No. 1 seed goes home. Alabama's season ends at the hands of No. 11 Texas Tech. What a wild WCWS. 😤 #RollTide #WCWS

research.facts:
Alabama Stunned — No. 1 Seed Eliminated by No. 11 Texas Tech
- **Classification:** FOLLOW UP
- **Freshness:** Season ended June 1.
- **Sources:**
- Alabama Athletics (rolltide.com) roster page and prior season-long coverage
- Yahoo Sports, ESPN
- **Key Facts (MEDIUM confidence — need verification on final record):**
- Alabama entered the WCWS as No. 1 overall seed with a strong record (approx. 54-9 final).
- Jocelyn Briski: SEC POY, 1.30 ERA, 23+ wins on the season.
- Brooke Wells: 23+ HRs, 65 RBI, led team offensively.
- Alabama's last national title: 2012. Had not returned to the finals since.
- Eliminated by No. 11 seed Texas Tech — one of the bigger upsets in recent WCWS history.
- **Confidence note:** Alabama's final record needs confirmation; using "54-9" from Alabama Athletics mid-tournament. Will mark MEDIUM for the article, not making it a centerpiece stat.
